aiUsage.title: AI Usage & Costs
aiUsage.limitReachedDesc: Monthly AI spending limit has been reached. AI features (summarization, transcription) are blocked. Increase the limit or wait for the next month.
aiUsage.limitWarningDesc: Monthly AI spending has reached {{percent}}% of the ${{limit}} limit.

landing.heroDesc: PR-TOP is an AI-powered practice assistant for psychologists and therapists — built to reduce routine, support continuity between sessions, and keep care human.
landing.featuresDesc: Every feature is built around the real workflow of mental health professionals: less routine, more continuity, and protection of your personal boundaries.
landing.pricingDesc: Start with a free trial. Upgrade as your practice grows.
landing.feature1Desc: Automatic session transcription and smart AI summaries. No more spending evenings on notes — documentation is generated for you, you just review.
landing.feature2Desc: All work history, context, and important details are reliably stored in one place. Even with a heavy caseload — no nuance is ever lost.
landing.feature3Desc: All records and client communications are encrypted. Only you have access — even the service team cannot read your data.
landing.feature4Desc: Configurable response windows and smart message triage. Your clients get support, and you get a peaceful evening and proper rest.
landing.feature5Desc: Dictate your thoughts between sessions — they're instantly transcribed and always at hand. Perfect for breaks between clients.
landing.feature6Desc: Clients interact through Telegram — no new apps to install. Diary entries, exercises, and SOS alerts in their existing messenger.
landing.burnoutTitle: We built this so you have more time for yourself
landing.burnoutText: Burnout and compassion fatigue are a reality of helping professions. You need time for proper rest and recovery. We take on the routine tasks, protect your personal boundaries, and free up time for life.
landing.faqQ1: Who can read my notes and client communications?
landing.faqA1: All confidential data is encrypted at the application level (AES-256). No one but you has access to the content — including the service support and development team.
landing.faqQ2: Is there an SOS button for clients to reach me urgently?
landing.faqA2: Yes. The built-in SOS alert system lets clients send an emergency signal directly through the Telegram bot. You'll receive an instant notification and can respond promptly.
landing.faqQ3: I only have a few clients and manage fine on my own. How can this help?
landing.faqA3: Even with a small caseload, the service handles the routine: transcriptions, notes, reminders. You get more free time. And as your practice grows — a ready infrastructure that scales with you.
landing.faqQ4: Are there reminder and messaging features for clients?
landing.faqA4: Yes. You can create follow-ups, session reminders, and support messages — quickly and conveniently. Your focus stays on therapy, not administration.
landing.faqQ5: Who will benefit most from the service?
landing.faqA5: Professionals who keep client histories, assign homework, and want to maintain contact between sessions. The more clients you have — the more time and energy the service saves.

guide.title: Therapist Guide
guide.subtitle: Step-by-step instructions for using all features of the service
guide.section1Body: The dashboard is your main workspace. Here you can see key statistics at a glance, recent client activity, and manage your invite code.
guide.section1Stat1: Active Clients — the number of clients currently linked to you
guide.section1Stat3: Notes — your private therapist notes count
guide.section1Stat4: Active SOS — currently unresolved SOS alerts from clients
guide.section1Activity: The activity feed shows recent diary entries, sessions, SOS events, and notes in chronological order. Each item shows the client name, type, and time.
guide.section1Invite: Your invite code is a unique code you share with clients so they can connect to you through the Telegram bot. You can copy it or regenerate a new one at any time.
guide.section1Badge: The subscription badge in the header shows your current plan (Trial, Basic, Pro, or Premium) and remaining trial days if applicable.
guide.section2Body: The Clients page displays all clients linked to your account. Use the search bar to filter by email or Telegram ID.
guide.section2Dot1: Green dot — client was active within the last day
guide.section2Dot2: Teal dot — client was active within the last 7 days
guide.section2Dot3: Amber dot — client was active within the last 30 days
guide.section2Dot4: Gray dot — client has been inactive for more than 30 days
guide.section2Consent: The consent status shows whether the client has granted permission for you to access their data. Consent can be granted or revoked by the client at any time. When consent is revoked, the client's data is hidden from all views including dashboard, search, and analytics.
guide.section2Open: Click on any client row to open their detailed profile with timeline, diary, notes, sessions, exercises, and context tabs.
guide.section3Body: Each client profile has six tabs giving you a complete view of the therapeutic relationship:
guide.section3Tab1Desc: A unified chronological view of all events — diary entries, sessions, notes, exercises, and SOS alerts. Filter by date range and event type.
guide.section3Tab2Desc: Client's diary entries including text, voice, and video messages. Filter by type and date range. Supports infinite scroll for large histories.
guide.section3Tab3Desc: Your private therapist notes. Create, edit, and delete notes about the client. These are only visible to you.
guide.section3Tab4Desc: Client context including anamnesis/history, current therapy goals, contraindications, and AI instructions. This information helps maintain continuity between sessions.
guide.section3Tab5Desc: List of recorded sessions with links to detailed views. Upload audio files for automatic transcription and AI-generated summaries.
guide.section3Tab6Desc: Assign exercises to the client from the exercise library. Track which exercises have been delivered and view client responses.
guide.section4Body: Sessions allow you to record and analyze therapy sessions. You can upload audio recordings for automatic transcription and AI-powered summarization.
guide.section4Step1: Upload — select and upload an audio file from your device
guide.section4Step2: Transcribing — the system automatically converts speech to text (this may take a few minutes)
guide.section4Step3: Complete — the transcript and AI-generated summary are ready for review
guide.section4Statuses: Each session shows its current status and whether audio, transcript, and summary are available. Click on a session to view full details.
guide.section5Body: The Exercise Library contains structured therapeutic exercises organized into six categories:
guide.section5Cat1: Breathing & Relaxation — breathing techniques and progressive relaxation exercises
guide.section5Cat4: Journaling — guided writing and reflection prompts
guide.section5Cat5: Behavioral — behavioral activation and habit-building exercises
guide.section5Assign: To assign an exercise to a client: go to the client's Exercises tab, click 'Send Exercise', browse or search the library, and select the exercise to send.
guide.section5bBody: In addition to the built-in exercise library, you can create your own custom exercises tailored to your therapeutic approach. Your custom exercises are visible only to you and your clients — other therapists cannot see them.
guide.section5bStep1Desc: Go to Exercise Library from the sidebar menu and click the 'My Exercises' tab at the top. Here you'll see all exercises you've created.
guide.section5bStep2Desc: Click the '+ Create Exercise' button. Fill in the form: choose a category (or create a custom one), enter a title, a brief description, and step-by-step instructions. You can add translations in English, Russian, and Spanish using the language tabs — a checkmark appears next to each language that has content.
guide.section5bStep3Desc: Find the exercise in your list and click the pencil (edit) icon. The form opens pre-filled with the current content. Make your changes and click 'Save Exercise'.
guide.section5bStep4Desc: Click the trash (delete) icon on the exercise card. A confirmation dialog will appear asking you to confirm. Note: you cannot delete an exercise that has already been sent to a client and is still in progress. Complete or cancel the active assignment first.
guide.section5bStep5Desc: Custom exercises are assigned the same way as built-in exercises. Go to your client's profile, open the Exercises tab, click 'Send Exercise', and select your custom exercise from the list. Your custom exercises are marked with a 'My' badge so you can easily identify them.
guide.section5bTip: Tip: Write clear, numbered instructions (one step per line) so your clients can follow along easily in their Telegram bot.
guide.section6Body: Clients can press the SOS button in their Telegram bot when they need urgent support. You will be notified through your configured channels:
guide.section6Channel1: Telegram notifications — receive alerts directly in Telegram
guide.section6Channel2: Email notifications — get email alerts for SOS events
guide.section6Channel3: Web push notifications — browser push notifications on desktop
guide.section6Channel4: Sound alert — audible notification on the dashboard
guide.section6Quiet: Quiet hours allow you to delay non-urgent notifications during specified times (e.g., nighttime). SOS alerts during quiet hours are held until the quiet period ends, with a configurable escalation delay.
guide.section6Recommend: When you receive an SOS alert, we recommend responding as soon as possible. The SOS section on the dashboard shows all active alerts requiring attention.
guide.section7Body: The Analytics page provides visual insights into client activity and your practice:
guide.section7Chart1: Activity by client — compare engagement levels across clients
guide.section7Chart2: Sessions by status — see the distribution of session statuses
guide.section7Chart3: Stacked activity timeline — diary entries, sessions, and notes plotted over time
guide.section7DateRange: Use the date range selector to focus on specific time periods. Hover over chart elements to see detailed tooltips with exact values.
guide.section8Body: Configure your account preferences and manage your subscription:
guide.section8Lang: Language — switch the interface between English, Russian, and Spanish
guide.section8Tz: Timezone — set your timezone for correct date/time display
guide.section8Plans: Subscription plans: Trial (14 days free), Basic, Pro, Premium — each with different client and session limits
guide.section8Upgrade: To upgrade your plan, go to the Subscription page and select your desired plan. Payment is handled securely through Stripe.
guide.section8History: View your payment history and manage billing on the Subscription page
guide.faq1Q: How does a client connect to me?
guide.faq1A: Share your unique invite code with your client. They enter it in the Telegram bot to link their account to yours. You can find and copy your invite code on the Dashboard.
guide.faq2Q: What happens if a client revokes consent?
guide.faq2A: If a client revokes consent, all their sensitive data becomes inaccessible to you: diary entries, session recordings, transcripts, summaries, exercises, SOS alerts, and search results. Their data is also excluded from your dashboard statistics, activity feed, and analytics. Your own therapist notes remain accessible. The link is preserved and access is fully restored when the client re-grants consent.
guide.faq3Q: How does SOS work at night (quiet hours)?
guide.faq3A: During quiet hours, non-urgent notifications are delayed. SOS alerts are held and delivered as soon as the quiet period ends, or after the configured escalation delay, whichever comes first.
guide.faq4A: Yes. All sensitive client data (diary entries, notes, transcripts, summaries) is encrypted at the application layer before being stored in the database. This means even database access does not expose raw client data.
guide.faq5Q: How do I change my subscription plan?
guide.faq5A: Go to the Subscription page from the sidebar. You can upgrade immediately or schedule a downgrade for the end of your current billing period. Payment is handled securely through Stripe.
guide.faq6Q: What happens if my subscription expires?
guide.faq6A: If your subscription expires, you will be redirected to the Subscription page to select a new plan. Your data is safely preserved and will be accessible again once you subscribe.
guide.faq7A: Yes! On any client's profile, click 'Export All Data' to download their complete data as JSON or CSV (ZIP). JSON includes profile, context, diary, sessions, notes, exercises, and SOS events. CSV bundles separate files in a ZIP archive. Trial/Basic plans can export diary data in JSON; Pro/Premium plans unlock full export with all data types and CSV format. Analytics can also be exported from the Analytics page.
guide.faq8A: Voice messages and audio recordings are processed through our speech-to-text pipeline. The audio is uploaded, transcribed automatically, and an AI-generated summary is created. The process typically takes a few minutes.
guide.faq9A: AI instructions in the client's Context tab allow you to set boundaries and guidelines for how AI processes that client's data. For example, you can specify topics to avoid or particular approaches to emphasize.
guide.faq10A: For technical support, reach out via the contact information on the landing page. We aim to respond to all inquiries within 24 hours.

pwa.installDesc: Install the app for a faster, native-like experience with offline support.
pwa.offline: You are offline. Some features may be unavailable.

security.heroSubtitle: Learn how PR-TOP protects your clients' most sensitive data with industry-leading security measures.

enc.atRestP1: All sensitive client data stored in our database is encrypted using AES-256, the same standard used by banks and government agencies. Each record is encrypted with a unique initialization vector, ensuring that even identical data produces different ciphertext.
enc.atRestP2: Database backups inherit the same encryption, so your data remains protected even in cold storage.
enc.inTransitP1: Every connection between your browser and PR-TOP servers is secured with TLS 1.3, the latest and most secure transport layer protocol. This prevents eavesdropping, tampering, and man-in-the-middle attacks.
enc.inTransitP2: We enforce HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) headers with a two-year duration to ensure your browser never accidentally downgrades to an unencrypted connection.
enc.appLayerP1: Beyond database-level encryption, PR-TOP applies a second layer of application-level encryption to all Class A sensitive data before it reaches the database. This means that even a database administrator cannot read the data without the application encryption keys. Class A data includes:
enc.appLayerItem1: Client diary entries (text, voice transcripts, video transcripts)
enc.appLayerItem3: Conversation messages between therapist and client
enc.appLayerItem5: SOS alert content and exercise responses
enc.keyMgmtP1: Encryption keys are derived from a master key that is stored separately from the database. The master key is never committed to source code and is injected at runtime through secure environment variables.
enc.keyMgmtP2: Key rotation procedures are supported to periodically refresh encryption without disrupting service availability.
enc.zeroKnowledgeP1: PR-TOP is designed so that the platform operators cannot access your clients' sensitive data. All encryption and decryption happens within the application layer, and only authorized therapists with valid authentication tokens can trigger decryption.
enc.zeroKnowledgeP2: Even in the event of a server breach, the attacker would only find encrypted ciphertext — not readable client data.

gdpr.minimizationP1: PR-TOP collects only the data strictly necessary for providing the therapeutic assistant service. We do not collect browsing habits, advertising identifiers, or any data unrelated to the therapist-client workflow.
gdpr.minimizationP2: Analytics are powered by Umami, a privacy-first, cookie-free analytics platform that collects no personal data and is fully GDPR-compliant.
gdpr.erasureP1: Clients and therapists can request complete data deletion at any time. When a deletion request is processed, all associated data — diary entries, session recordings, transcripts, notes, and AI-generated summaries — is permanently and irreversibly removed from the database.
gdpr.erasureP2: Encrypted backups are rotated on a schedule, ensuring that deleted data does not persist in backup storage beyond the retention window.
gdpr.consentP1: PR-TOP implements granular consent controls that give clients full authority over their data:
gdpr.consentItem1: Clients can grant or revoke therapist access to their data at any time via Telegram
gdpr.consentItem2: When consent is revoked, all client data becomes immediately inaccessible to the therapist — including diary entries, sessions, search, dashboard statistics, and analytics
gdpr.consentItem3: Consent status is checked in real-time on every data access request, with full audit logging of consent changes
gdpr.dpaP1: For organizations and clinics, PR-TOP provides a Data Processing Agreement template that outlines the responsibilities of both parties regarding personal data handling.
gdpr.dpaP2: The DPA covers data processing purposes, security measures, sub-processor agreements, breach notification procedures, and data subject rights.
gdpr.processingP1: PR-TOP supports deployment within the European Union to ensure that data never leaves EU jurisdictions. This satisfies the GDPR requirement for lawful data transfers.
gdpr.processingP2: For self-hosted deployments, therapists have complete control over where their data is physically stored and processed.

audit.eventsP1: PR-TOP maintains a comprehensive audit trail of all security-relevant events. Every action that touches sensitive data is recorded with a timestamp, actor identity, and action description:
audit.eventsItem1: Authentication events: login, logout, failed login attempts, password resets
audit.eventsItem2: Data access: client record views, diary entry reads, session transcript access
audit.eventsItem3: Data modifications: record creation, updates, deletions
audit.eventsItem4: Consent changes: grant, revoke, and consent-denied access attempts
audit.eventsItem5: Administrative actions: user role changes, system configuration updates, AI spending limit changes
audit.retentionP1: Audit logs are retained for a configurable period (default: 90 days) to balance accountability with storage efficiency. Critical security events (failed logins, consent changes, data deletions) are retained for a longer period.
audit.retentionP2: Automated cleanup runs on a scheduled basis, removing only logs that have exceeded their retention window.
audit.tamperProofP1: Audit log entries are append-only — once written, they cannot be modified or deleted through the application interface. This ensures the integrity of the audit trail even if an account is compromised.
audit.tamperProofP2: Logs are stored in a dedicated database table with restricted write access, separate from the main application data.
audit.transparencyP1: Therapists can view the audit trail for their own account, seeing exactly when and how their client data was accessed. This transparency builds trust and supports accountability in the therapeutic relationship.
audit.transparencyP2: Superadmins have access to the full system audit log through the admin dashboard, with filtering by event type, user, and date range.

ds.residencyP1: PR-TOP supports configurable data residency to meet regional compliance requirements. You can choose to host your data in the European Union or the United States, with the guarantee that data stays within your chosen jurisdiction.
ds.residencyP2: No data is transferred across borders without explicit configuration by the platform administrator.
ds.selfHostedP1: For maximum control, PR-TOP offers a Docker-based self-hosted deployment option. You run the entire platform — application server, database, AI services, and analytics — on your own infrastructure.
ds.selfHostedP2: Self-hosting ensures that your data never touches third-party servers. You control the hardware, the network, the backups, and the encryption keys.
ds.noThirdPartyP1: PR-TOP never sells, shares, or provides client data to third parties. AI processing (transcription, summarization) uses API calls where only the minimum necessary data is sent, and no data is retained by AI providers after processing.
ds.noThirdPartyP2: We do not use client data for model training, advertising, or any purpose beyond providing the requested therapeutic assistant features.
ds.clientOwnershipP1: Clients own their data. They can export their diary entries and exercise responses at any time, and they can request complete data deletion. Therapists maintain ownership of their own notes and session recordings.
ds.clientOwnershipP2: PR-TOP acts as a data processor, not a data controller — the therapist and client remain the decision-makers about how their data is used.

dataCollection.title: What Data We Collect
dataCollection.intro: PR-TOP collects only the data necessary to provide its therapist assistant services. We distinguish between the following categories:
dataCollection.personalDesc: When you register, we collect your email address, name, and chosen password (stored as a cryptographic hash). Therapists may also provide a practice name and professional credentials.
dataCollection.clinicalDesc: Clients may submit diary entries (text, voice messages, video messages), exercise responses, and SOS alerts through Telegram. Therapists may upload session recordings, write private notes, and create AI-assisted summaries. All clinical data is classified as Class A sensitive data and encrypted at the application layer.
dataCollection.usageDesc: We collect anonymized usage analytics through Umami, a privacy-first analytics platform that uses no cookies and stores no personal identifiers. This includes page views, traffic sources, and device types — but never individual user behavior.

dataUsage.title: How We Use Your Data
dataUsage.intro: Your data is used exclusively to provide and improve the PR-TOP therapeutic assistant service:
dataUsage.item1: Providing therapist-client workflows: diary access, session management, exercise delivery, SOS alerts
dataUsage.item2: AI-powered features: speech-to-text transcription, session summarization, semantic search, natural language queries
dataUsage.item3: Account management: authentication, subscription billing, email notifications
dataUsage.item4: Service improvement: anonymized aggregate analytics to understand feature usage patterns
dataUsage.noAds: We never use your data for advertising, user profiling, or model training purposes.

dataStorage.title: Data Storage & Encryption
dataStorage.intro: PR-TOP employs multiple layers of protection to safeguard your data:
dataStorage.classADesc: Diary entries, session transcripts, therapist notes, AI summaries, SOS content, and exercise responses are encrypted at the application layer using AES-256 before being stored in the database. Even database administrators cannot read this data without the application encryption keys.
dataStorage.classBDesc: Timestamps, user role information, subscription status, and scheduling metadata are access-controlled but may remain as plaintext in the database. Payment data is handled entirely by Stripe and never touches our servers.
dataStorage.transit: All data in transit is protected with TLS 1.3 encryption. We enforce HSTS headers with a two-year duration to prevent connection downgrades.

thirdParties.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParties.intro: PR-TOP integrates with the following third-party services to provide its features:
thirdParties.stripe: Payment processing for subscriptions. Stripe handles all payment card data in compliance with PCI DSS standards. We never store card numbers on our servers.
thirdParties.aiDesc: Transcription and summarization services (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, Google). Only the minimum necessary content is sent for processing, and AI providers do not retain your data after processing is complete.
thirdParties.telegram: Client-facing messaging interface. Telegram delivers messages between clients and the bot; message content is encrypted in our database upon receipt.
thirdParties.noSale: We never sell, rent, or trade your personal or clinical data to any third party, for any reason.

dataRetention.title: Data Retention & Deletion
dataRetention.intro: We retain your data only for as long as you maintain an active account and have an ongoing therapeutic relationship that requires it.
dataRetention.deletion: You may request complete deletion of your account and all associated data at any time. Upon receiving a deletion request, we permanently and irreversibly remove all personal information, clinical data, session recordings, transcripts, notes, and AI-generated content from our active database.
dataRetention.backups: Encrypted backups are rotated on a scheduled basis. Deleted data will be purged from backup storage within the backup retention window (typically 30 days).

cookies.title: Cookies & Tracking
cookies.intro: PR-TOP is designed with a privacy-first approach to analytics and tracking.
cookies.umami: We use Umami for web analytics — a self-hosted, open-source analytics platform that is fully GDPR-compliant. Umami does not use cookies, does not track individual users, and does not collect personal data. All analytics data is aggregated and anonymized.
cookies.essential: We use only essential browser storage (localStorage) for authentication tokens and language preferences. No third-party tracking cookies, advertising pixels, or fingerprinting technologies are used.

children.title: Children's Privacy
children.desc: PR-TOP is a professional tool designed for licensed therapists and their adult clients. Our service is intended for users aged 18 and older. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 18. If you believe a minor has provided us with personal data, please contact us immediately so we can delete it.

contact.title: Contact Information
contact.desc: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, your data, or wish to exercise your rights (access, correction, deletion, data portability), please contact us at:

updates.title: Updates to This Policy
updates.desc: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices, technology, or legal requirements. The "Last updated" date at the top of this page indicates when the policy was last revised.
updates.notification: For material changes, we will notify registered users via email and/or an in-app notification at least 14 days before the changes take effect. Continued use of PR-TOP after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.p1: By accessing or using PR-TOP (the "Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you do not agree to these Terms, you may not access or use the Service.
acceptance.p2: These Terms constitute a legally binding agreement between you and PR-TOP. By creating an account, you represent that you are at least 18 years old and have the legal capacity to enter into this agreement.

service.title: Description of Service
service.p1: PR-TOP is a therapist-controlled between-session assistant platform designed for practicing psychologists and therapists. The Service provides tools for client diary management, session transcription and summarization, exercise delivery, SOS alerting, and practice analytics.
service.p2: The Service operates through a web dashboard for therapists and a Telegram bot interface for clients. AI-powered features include speech-to-text transcription, session summarization, semantic search, and natural language queries.
service.p3: PR-TOP strives to maintain reasonable uptime and availability. While we aim for high reliability, we do not guarantee uninterrupted access. Scheduled maintenance windows will be communicated in advance when possible.

accounts.title: User Accounts & Registration
accounts.p1: Therapist accounts are created through the web registration form. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activities that occur under your account.
accounts.p2: Client access is granted exclusively through therapist-generated invite codes. Clients interact with the Service through the Telegram bot and do not have direct web dashboard access.
accounts.p3: You must provide accurate and complete registration information. You agree to promptly update your account information if it changes. We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that provide false or misleading information.

acceptableUse.title: Acceptable Use Policy
acceptableUse.intro: You agree to use the Service only for its intended purpose and in compliance with all applicable laws and professional ethics standards. You must not:
acceptableUse.item1: Use the Service for any unlawful purpose or to facilitate illegal activities
acceptableUse.item2: Attempt to gain unauthorized access to other users' accounts, data, or system resources
acceptableUse.item3: Upload malicious code, viruses, or any content designed to disrupt the Service
acceptableUse.item4: Use the Service to store or process data unrelated to legitimate therapeutic practice
acceptableUse.item5: Misrepresent your professional qualifications, credentials, or licensure status

subscriptions.title: Subscriptions & Payments
subscriptions.p1: PR-TOP offers subscription tiers (Trial, Basic, Pro, Premium) with varying feature sets and usage limits. Pricing and tier details are displayed on our website and may be updated from time to time.
subscriptions.p2: Payments are processed by Stripe, our third-party payment processor. By subscribing, you agree to Stripe's terms of service. All payment card data is handled by Stripe in compliance with PCI DSS — we never store card numbers on our servers.
subscriptions.p3: Subscriptions renew automatically at the end of each billing period unless cancelled. You may cancel your subscription at any time through the account settings. Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period; no partial refunds are provided for unused time.
subscriptions.p4: We may offer a free trial period for new accounts. If you do not cancel before the trial expires, your account may be downgraded or you may be charged for the selected plan, depending on the terms presented at signup.

ip.title: Intellectual Property
ip.p1: The PR-TOP platform, including its software, design, logos, documentation, and all related intellectual property, is owned by PR-TOP and protected by applicable copyright and trademark laws. You may not copy, modify, distribute, or reverse-engineer any part of the platform.
ip.p2: Therapists retain full ownership of all content they create through the Service, including private notes, session recordings, and any materials uploaded to the platform. PR-TOP claims no ownership over therapist-created content.
ip.p3: Clients retain ownership of their personal data, including diary entries, exercise responses, and voice/video messages. The therapist-client data relationship is governed by the professional therapeutic agreement between the therapist and client, not by PR-TOP.

dataPrivacy.title: Data Privacy
dataPrivacy.p1: The protection of your personal and clinical data is fundamental to our Service. We employ application-layer encryption (AES-256) for all sensitive clinical data (Class A), TLS 1.3 for data in transit, and strict access controls throughout the platform.
dataPrivacy.p2: For complete details on how we collect, use, store, and protect your data, please refer to our

disclaimers.title: Disclaimers
disclaimers.p1: PR-TOP IS NOT A MEDICAL DEVICE, DIAGNOSTIC TOOL, OR EMERGENCY SERVICE.
disclaimers.p2: The Service is a practice management and between-session support tool for licensed therapists. It does not provide medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. All clinical decisions remain the sole responsibility of the licensed therapist.
disclaimers.p3: The SOS alert feature is a notification relay system that alerts the therapist — it is not a substitute for emergency services. In case of immediate danger, users should contact local emergency services (911, 112, or equivalent).
disclaimers.p4: AI-generated content (transcriptions, summaries, search results) is provided as a convenience and may contain errors. Therapists must verify AI outputs before relying on them for clinical decisions.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.p1: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, PR-TOP AND ITS OFFICERS, DIRECTORS, EMPLOYEES, AND AGENTS SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO YOUR USE OF THE SERVICE.
liability.p2: Our total aggregate liability for any claims arising under these Terms shall not exceed the amount you paid to PR-TOP in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

indemnification.title: Indemnification
indemnification.p1: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless PR-TOP and its affiliates from any claims, damages, losses, liabilities, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or related to: (a) your use of the Service; (b) your violation of these Terms; (c) your violation of any applicable law or regulation; or (d) any content you submit through the Service.

termination.title: Termination
termination.p1: You may terminate your account at any time by contacting support or using the account deletion feature. Upon termination, your data will be handled in accordance with our Privacy Policy.
termination.p2: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account if you violate these Terms, engage in fraudulent activity, or if required by law. We will provide reasonable notice when possible, except in cases of severe violations or legal requirements.
termination.p3: Upon termination, you may request export of your data (therapist notes, session records) in a portable format, subject to our data retention policy. Client data export requests should be directed through the client's own account.

governingLaw.title: Governing Law
governingLaw.p1: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the jurisdiction in which PR-TOP is incorporated, without regard to its conflict-of-law provisions. Any disputes arising from these Terms or the Service shall be resolved through binding arbitration or in the courts of the applicable jurisdiction.

changes.title: Changes to These Terms
changes.p1: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. The "Last updated" date at the top of this page indicates when the Terms were last revised.
changes.p2: For material changes, we will provide at least 30 days' notice via email and/or prominent notice within the Service. Your continued use of the Service after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the modified Terms. If you do not agree with the updated Terms, you must stop using the Service and terminate your account.

contact.title: Contact Information
contact.desc: If you have any questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us at:

assistant.title: Assistant
assistant.hint: Ask me about using PR-TOP — navigation, features, workflows, and more.
